techcrunch.title: OpenAI launches its new family of models with GPT-5.6
techcrunch.description: OpenAI's latest family of models promise improvements across a range of areas, including cybersecurity.

techcrunch.title: An AI agent startup just let its agent run its $100 million fundraise
techcrunch.description: Lyzr, a startup that builds AI agents for enterprises, used its own AI agent to raise a $100 million round — proof, evidently, that the product actually works.

techcrunch.title: OpenAI is shutting down Atlas, but its AI browser ambitions are still growing
techcrunch.description: OpenAI is sunsetting its AI-powered browser after less than a year. But it's moving some agentic browsing features to its desktop app and a Chrome extension.

techcrunch.title: Elon Musk praises Mythos/Fable, promises not to ‘cut off’ Anthropic
techcrunch.description: Should Anthropic trust Elon Musk to host its models? With about $40 billion in revenue at stake, Musk insists that the company can.

techcrunch.title: Can AI answer the $3 trillion question?
techcrunch.description: The AI ROI debate has returned and the numbers are even bigger, as are, perhaps, the consequences.

techcrunch.title: Meta enters the crowded AI coding battle with Muse Spark 1.1
techcrunch.description: Meta's pitch to users is Spark's ability to handle large agentic workloads, fix bugs, and help with large code migrations — the kind of automation that enterprises are increasingly turning to AI companies to provide.

techcrunch.title: Charles Hudson shares the common mistakes he’s seen after investing in 500+ startups
techcrunch.description: In this week’s episode of Build Mode,  Isabelle Johannessen talks with Precursor Ventures' Charles Hudson about the headwinds facing early-stage founders today and the most common mistakes founders should avoid in order to get funded.

techcrunch.title: New York Times says OpenAI hid evidence in ChatGPT copyright trial
techcrunch.description: News publishers say OpenAI hid tools and datasets that could identify copyrighted journalism in ChatGPT outputs, escalating their lawsuit with a new motion for sanctions.

techcrunch.title: Slate Auto teams up with Crayola to color its EV truck
techcrunch.description: Slate has an answer for owners who have always want to drive a truck with bright crayon colors.

techcrunch.title: FanDuel sent a video from star athlete Bryce Harper to a customer with a gambling problem
techcrunch.description: The video call highlights the murky relationship between professional athletes and gambling apps.

techcrunch.title: Mercor is in talks for a $20B valuation
techcrunch.description: A new $20 billion valuation would be a giant step up from the $10 billion valuation it reached in October.

techcrunch.title: Google will now disclose which ads are made with AI
techcrunch.description: While Google prohibits misleading and deceptive ads, an ad can still leverage AI to create some type of synthetic or digitally altered content. Until now, that's something Google only required election ads to disclose.

techcrunch.title: Paris-based AI voice startup Gradium raises $100M seed, backed by Nvidia
techcrunch.description: The company is using the cash to open an office in the Bay Area and compete for talent there, "strengthening its position at the heart of the world's leading AI ecosystem."

techcrunch.title: How did the government decide OpenAI’s frontier model was safe to release?
techcrunch.description: "Exactly what that dialog looked like between the government and Anthropic and OpenAI is unclear."

techcrunch.title: Instagram users: Here’s how to stop Meta’s AI from using your photos
techcrunch.description: Muse Image allows users to generate AI images using photos from public Instagram accounts. As long as a person's profile is public, another user can tag that account and use their images as part of an AI-generated creation.

techcrunch.title: Meta’s new AI chips will begin production in September
techcrunch.description: The company is taking a modular approach to designing these chips, anticipating that their needs will change as AI evolves rapidly by the time the chips are in production.

techcrunch.title: Nvidia is a victim of the compute marketplace it created
techcrunch.description: Having proven how valuable compute can be, the company finds itself at the center of a market everyone wants to be in — while simpler technologies and less interesting companies get rich on the sidelines.

techcrunch.title: Block reaches $45M settlement with 46 states over Cash App fraud probe
techcrunch.description: State attorneys general said they found that Block misled users by falsely advertising that Cash App provided bank-like protections, including advanced fraud detection.

techcrunch.title: Anthropic’s new Claude feature is quietly selling you on AI
techcrunch.description: Claude’s new Reflect dashboard doesn’t just visualize how you use AI. It also subtly reinforces how much of your daily work now depends on Anthropic’s chatbot.

techcrunch.title: Anthropic, OpenAI, and SpaceX are bigger than the last 25 years of tech exits
techcrunch.description: Three big AI IPOs are set to generate more value than all the U.S. VC-backed exits since 2000.
